content.text:
Done. The fix is pushed to PR #568.

**What I did:**
1. Removed the orphaned `cosmo.gemini.enable = false;` line (line 3) from `home/identities/work.nix` — the option was deleted from `home/dev.nix` in this PR but the assignment lingered, breaking every standalone home config that imports the work identity.
2. Re-grepped the repo: no `cosmo.gemini` or `gemini-cli` references remain. The only surviving `gemini` hit is the unrelated `gemini-code-assist[bot]` trusted-reviewer line in `home/dev.nix`, which I left untouched.
3. Verified the previously-failing config now evaluates: `nix eval .#homeConfigurations."paflynn@bushmills".activationPackage.drvPath` succeeds, no `cosmo.gemini` error.

Commit `879edb5` pushed to `feat/antigravity-native`; pre-commit hooks (nixfmt, etc.) passed.

result:
Done. The fix is pushed to PR #568.

**What I did:**
1. Removed the orphaned `cosmo.gemini.enable = false;` line (line 3) from `home/identities/work.nix` — the option was deleted from `home/dev.nix` in this PR but the assignment lingered, breaking every standalone home config that imports the work identity.
2. Re-grepped the repo: no `cosmo.gemini` or `gemini-cli` references remain. The only surviving `gemini` hit is the unrelated `gemini-code-assist[bot]` trusted-reviewer line in `home/dev.nix`, which I left untouched.
3. Verified the previously-failing config now evaluates: `nix eval .#homeConfigurations."paflynn@bushmills".activationPackage.drvPath` succeeds, no `cosmo.gemini` error.

Commit `879edb5` pushed to `feat/antigravity-native`; pre-commit hooks (nixfmt, etc.) passed.
